articles.title: Free glaucoma screening camp and awareness session at SHO Brodipet, Guntur on April 12
articles.description: Free glaucoma screening camp at SHO building in Brodipet, Guntur on Sunday; ophthalmologists to offer consultations and awareness session on eye health

articles.title: SC steps in on plea of Indians ‘stuck’ in Russia, ‘forced’ into war with Ukraine
articles.description: “We are stuck in Russia, fighting for a foreign state unwillingly… We could be either dead or injured. We are in an extreme situation”

articles.title: Middle East conflict: Why is Bahrain leading the campaign to reopen Strait of Hormuz?
articles.description: Bahrain's Foreign Minister warned that the Strait of Hormuz crisis threatens global stability, potentially affecting millions in the Arab world. He condemned the blockage of a Bahrain-backed UN resolution, emphasizing that the situation poses a direct threat to the Global South and global peace.

articles.title: Electric vehicle fined in Rajasthan for ‘no pollution certificate’; video goes viral
articles.description: The video shows a man arguing with a police official after being fined for having blacked-out windows, with the man claiming that these were merely car window sunshades and that the law does not prohibit their use.

articles.title: Morning vs. evening: how do the benefits of yoga differ depending on when you do it
articles.description: Participants who practised yoga in the morning reported a greater reduction in sleep disturbances and a shift towards ‘morningness’ — a preference for early-day activity aligned with natural biological rhythms, according to a new study

articles.title: Supreme Court refuses to entertain bail plea of suspended Punjab Police DIG in corruption case
articles.description: While dismissing his bail plea, the high court had granted him liberty to apply for bail afresh before the special court after the examination of material witnesses in the case

topStories.title: Wildfires Race Across US as Drought Spans Half the Nation
topStories.description: An unusually hot, snowless winter has fueled a fast start to the spring fire season across large swaths of the western, central and southeastern US, disrupting businesses and upending daily life.

topStories.title: Kamal Kharazi, former Iranian foreign minister, dies week after he was injured in US-Israeli strikes
topStories.description: Kamal Kharazi, former Iranian foreign minister, died from injuries sustained in US-Israeli airstrikes on April 1. He was 81 and head of the Strategic Council for International Relations. He previously represented Iran at the UN in New York and later served as foreign minister from 1997 to 2005

topStories.title: Pakistan preps for make-or-break Washington-Tehran meeting - but will Iran show up? All you need to know
topStories.description: Pakistan is preparing to host talks between US and Iranian delegations on Saturday, though Iran's participation is uncertain due to recent Israeli strikes in Lebanon. The agenda includes Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz, with discussions likely to be indirect.

dailyNews.title: With Iran Setting Limits, Strait of Hormuz Remains Thorny Politically
dailyNews.description: Even after a cease-fire, Iran is keeping a chokehold on traffic, forcing countries to cut deals that could put them at odds with the U.S.

dailyNews.description: Higher fuel costs are raising food and travel prices, while a shaky stock market tamps down free spenders.

dailyNews.description: Investors are betting that the European Central Bank and the Bank of England will raise interest rates this year.

dailyNews.title: Tracking 7 of Mamdani’s Biggest Campaign Promises
dailyNews.description: Zohran Mamdani based his successful campaign for mayor of New York City on several central pledges. Here’s an early look on where those stand.

dailyNews.title: How Airlines Turned First-Class Seats From Freebies to a Profit Engine
dailyNews.description: Airlines used to give away most of their nicest seats, but they have increasingly found ways to persuade people to pay a lot for them.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Changes Lock Some Employers Out of H-1B Visa Program
dailyNews.description: Since imposing a $100,000 fee on new visas in September, the Trump administration has upended the skilled worker program.

dailyNews.title: Judge Rejects Hegseth’s Second Attempt to Restrict Reporters at Pentagon
dailyNews.description: A federal judge gutted a set of rules that were adopted after the court declared an earlier press policy unconstitutional, in a case brought by The New York Times.

dailyNews.title: New Charter Allows RFK Jr. to Reclaim Vaccine Policy Despite Court Ruling
dailyNews.description: The charter, published on Thursday, alters the makeup and purpose of the panel, opening the door for Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to reclaim his revision of national vaccine policy.

topStories.title: Melania Trump Says She Was Not Associated With Jeffrey Epstein
topStories.description: Responding to what she said were smears, the first lady said she never had knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse and was not a victim of his. She called for a congressional hearing for his victims.

topStories.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed despite a ceasefire deal, with 800 tankers among 3,200 vessels backed up and mariners stranded.

topStories.description: Melania Trump shocked reporters with a live White House speech denying ties to Jeffrey Epstein, calling fake images and smears politically motivated.

dailyNews.title: 4/9: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: President Trump says he asked Netanyahu to scale back Lebanon strikes; Melania Trump makes a rare statement denying relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.

dailyNews.title: Trump criticizes NATO after talks with Rutte
dailyNews.description: President Trump bashed NATO after meeting with Secretary General Mark Rutte on Wednesday. Ken Thomas, national politics reporter for the Wall Street Journal and Dave Levinthal, senior editor for NOTUS, joined "The Takeout" to discuss that and some of the day's other top political headlines.

dailyNews.title: White House staff received email warning not to place bets on prediction markets
dailyNews.description: White House aides got an email last month telling them not to place bets on prediction markets with nonpublic information, multiple administration officials told CBS News.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.

dailyNews.title: A mother mourning the son she lost in the Iran war says she told Trump: 'Go get 'em'
dailyNews.description: More than a month after her son, Maj. Cody Khork, died in the Iran war, Donna Burhans has found a routine that helps her cope.

dailyNews.title: Exclusive: Cuban president reacts to Trump admin’s call for him to step down
dailyNews.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel says "stepping down [is] not part of our vocabulary" amid reports that the U.S. government has asked the Cuban leader to resign.

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: Trump says Iran 'better not' collect Strait of Hormuz toll
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israeli strikes.

dailyNews.title: Trump blasts MAGA influencers who have split with him over Iran
dailyNews.description: Trump posts a long critique of several right-wing media figures who have backed him in the past but have been outspoken in their objection to the Iran war.
